# Callbook — animated playbooks, written in DBN (Dodgeball Notation)

A chess-style notation for dodgeball plays that compiles to animated overhead
court diagrams. Plain JS, no build, no dependencies.

## Read first
- Spec (exact token semantics + EBNF): https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/NOTATION.md
- Agent guide (window.DBNEditor automation API, test ids, deep links): https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/DRIVING.md
- Glossary of dodgeball terms: https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/GLOSSARY.md

## Machine surfaces
- Render arbitrary notation: https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/?dbn=<url-encoded DBN>
- Play files (plain text): https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/examples/<id>.dbn
  ids: pitch-back three-ball insides insides-3 outsides outsides-3 kill-left
  kill-left-3 kill-right kill-right-3 home away mirror middle
- Every play is an anchor on one page: https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/#<id>
- Engine (two script tags, auto-mounts [data-db-play-dbn] elements):
  https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/vendor/play-animator.js
  https://iamnotsam.com/dodgeball-play-notation/vendor/dbn.js
- JS API: DBN.parse(text) -> play JSON; DodgeballPlay.mount(el, play, opts) -> controller
  {play, pause, seek, next, prev, replay, loop, destroy, el}

## Live examples
- Overview / how to adopt: https://iamnotsam.com/callbook/
- A real team's playbook: https://iamnotsam.com/dodging-playbook/
- Neutral demo playbook: https://iamnotsam.com/playbook-example/
- Quiz built on the same files: https://iamnotsam.com/playbook-quiz/

## Source
- https://github.com/storypixel/dodgeball-play-notation (canonical: notation, engine, spec, tests)
- https://github.com/storypixel/dodgeball-play-animator (mirror + scroll-through viewer)

House rule: no magic — every engine capability ships as notation token + spec
entry + test, or not at all. The (x,y) escape always works.
